originalTitle: Bowen: Trump has called for an Iran uprising but the lessons from Iraq in 1991 loom large
title: Hi Friends! Let's Talk About Important News Far Away!
content:
Hello from Africa! Your News Friend is Here!

Hello, my wonderful friends across Africa! It's me, your friendly news reporter, ready to tell you about something important happening far, far away. We're going to talk about big countries and their leaders. It might sound tricky, but we will make it super easy to understand. Ready for an adventure in news? Let's go! This news is about leaders and countries, and how sometimes, things can get a little bit tricky between them. But don't worry, we'll explain everything with simple words. Our main goal is always to have peace and for everyone to be kind. So let's learn together!

What is an "Uprising"?

Sometimes, people in a country are not happy. They might not like what their leaders are doing. When many, many people want a big change, we call it an 'uprising'. It means people are standing up for what they believe in. It's like when you and your friends agree on something really strongly! They might want their country to be run in a different way. They might want different rules. An uprising is when a lot of people come together to say, "We want things to change!" They hope their leaders will listen to them. Did you know? In many countries, people can vote for their leaders. This is a way to choose who they want to lead them. It's like choosing who gets to be the captain of your team!

Meet President Trump of America

Far, far away across the big ocean, there is a very big country called America. America has a leader, just like your country has a leader. The leader of America is called President Trump. He is a very important person. President Trump talks a lot about what he thinks is important for America and for other countries too. He makes big decisions. Sometimes, his decisions can affect many people around the world. Did you know? The President of America lives in a very famous house called the White House. It's a very big and special place!

A Country Called Iran

Now, let's learn about another country. This country is called Iran. It is also far away from us, and from America. Iran has its own leaders and its own way of doing things. The people in Iran have their own lives and their own dreams, just like you. Sometimes, countries don't always agree with each other. America and Iran have had some big disagreements for a long, long time. It's like when two friends can't agree on which game to play. But for countries, it's a much bigger problem. Did you know? Iran is a country that has a very, very long history! People have lived there for thousands of years. That's a very long time!

What President Trump Said

Recently, President Trump said something about Iran. He said that he thinks the people in Iran should have an "uprising." Remember what an uprising is? It's when people stand up to their leaders because they want big changes. So, President Trump was saying he wants the people in Iran to stand up. He wants them to tell their leaders they want things to be different. He thinks this would be a good thing for Iran. He believes that if the people of Iran stand up, it might make things better in their country. He thinks it might also make things better between America and Iran. This is what the news is all about. Did you know? Leaders from different countries often talk to each other to try and solve problems. This is called diplomacy, and it's a very important way to keep peace.

A Long Time Ago in Iraq

Now, let's talk about something that happened a long time ago. It was in a country called Iraq. Iraq is another country that is near Iran. A very long time ago, in 1991, there was a big problem in Iraq. Back then, there was a lot of fighting, which we call a 'war'. Some people in Iraq tried to stand up against their leader, a bit like an uprising. They hoped things would change quickly. But the fighting became very, very difficult to stop. It lasted for a long time. Many people got hurt. Many homes and schools were broken. It was a very sad time for everyone in Iraq. Did you know? A 'war' is when countries or groups of people fight using armies and weapons. It's the most serious kind of disagreement.

Why is it Hard to Stop Fighting?

The story of Iraq in 1991 teaches us a very important lesson. It teaches us that it is much, much easier to start fighting than it is to stop it. Once a war begins, it's like a big, runaway train. Many people get involved. It's not just two people fighting anymore. Many families are affected. Children cannot go to school. People have to leave their homes. It makes everyone sad and scared. Stopping a war takes a very long time. It takes a lot of talking and a lot of work to make peace again. That's why leaders are always careful about starting any kind of big problem or fight. Did you know? Peace is when there is no fighting or war. Everyone can live happily and safely. That is what we all hope for!

Why Talking is Better Than Fighting

So, when President Trump talks about an uprising in Iran, some smart people remember the lesson from Iraq. They remember that even if you want to help people, starting a big problem can lead to even bigger problems. It's better to find ways to talk and solve things. Imagine if you have a disagreement with your friend. What do you do? Do you start to fight and yell? Or do you sit down and talk about it? Usually, talking helps you understand each other better and find a happy solution. For countries, talking is called 'diplomacy'. It means leaders sit down and discuss their problems. They try to find solutions that make everyone happy, or at least help them agree not to fight. Did you know? Many wise people around the world believe that talking and understanding each other is always the best way to solve problems, not fighting.

Let's Hope for Peace!

So, our news today is about a big idea: it's easy to wish for changes, but it's very hard to stop a big fight once it starts. The lesson from Iraq helps us remember that. We hope that leaders everywhere, in America, in Iran, and all around the world, will always choose to talk. We hope they will choose peace and kindness. This way, children like you can grow up in a happy and safe world. Thank you for listening, my wonderful friends! Stay curious and kind, and I'll see you next time with more news from our amazing world!
summary: President Trump of America wants people in Iran to stand up for big changes, but wise people remember a lesson from a long time ago in Iraq, which teaches us that starting big problems like wars is much easier than making them stop.
whyMatters: This is important because when leaders talk about big changes or even fighting, it can affect many people and countries. It teaches us that talking and making peace is always better than starting a fight, because stopping a fight is very, very hard once it begins.

title: Countries agree to record release of emergency oil reserves as prices surge
content: The G7 group of nations welcomes the idea of releasing oil in response to the surge in prices since the US-Israel war with Iran began

originalTitle: Havertz scores late penalty to earn Arsenal a draw at Leverkusen

originalTitle: Spurs keeper Kinsky thankful for support after 'nightmare'
title: Spurs keeper Kinsky thankful for support after 'nightmare'
content: Antonin Kinsky thanks those who have sent messages of support after a "nightmare" display in Spurs' Champions League defeat at Atletico Madrid.

originalTitle: Kinsky chaos as Atletico put five past sorry Spurs in Madrid
title: Kinsky chaos as Atletico put five past sorry Spurs in Madrid
content: Tottenham's troubles go from bad to worse as they're beaten 5-2 by Atletico Madrid in the first leg of their last 16 tie in the Champions League, with Spurs goalkeeper Antonin Kinsky substituted after just 17 minutes, with his team already 3-0 down at that stage.
